rationale: The citing paper gives Srinivasan, Vanhuele, and Pauwels (2010) a clear functional role in its literature framing: it is presented as a key prior model that links intermediate consumer mind-set measures to sales. Hu, Du, and Damangir then explicitly position their contribution as different from and advancing that stream by modeling search interest and conversion dynamics, rather than survey mind-sets, in a new context (automobiles). This is more than a passing mention because the cited paper helps define the conceptual baseline that the new decomposition approach is built to move beyond.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the cited paper's intermediate-metrics market response line by shifting from survey-based mind-set metrics to online search-volume measures and by decomposing advertising effects into interest generation versus conversion. It uses the cited study as a direct prior benchmark for positioning its added modeling and contextual contribution.
